headline_summary: U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran have escalated into a multi-day regional conflict with direct impacts on energy infrastructure and shipping.
Iran has declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and warned it will fire on any ship attempting to pass; multiple vessels have been hit.
Major producers and facilities have taken precautionary shutdowns (Qatar LNG, Saudi Ras Tanura refinery, Israeli gas fields, Iraqi Kurdistan output), tightening supply.
Container lines (Maersk, Hapag-Lloyd, CMA CGM, MSC) are rerouting/pausing Suez/Bab el-Mandeb transits and halting Hormuz crossings; war-risk surcharges and insurer cover cancellations add costs.
Oil, European gas and shipping rates have surged; broader global markets have moved risk-off amid inflation and growth concerns.
risk_drivers: Threatened/prospective closure of the Strait of Hormuz (critical chokepoint for oil/LNG)
Direct attacks on tankers and energy infrastructure across Gulf states
Precautionary shutdowns at major oil/gas facilities (Qatar LNG, Saudi refinery, Israeli gas fields, Iraqi production)
Spillover into Lebanon/Hezbollah front raising multi-theatre escalation risk
War-risk insurance withdrawal and extreme freight-rate repricing disrupting physical markets

Energy: Higher oil risk premium; potential for further upside if Hormuz closure persists or more Gulf infrastructure is hit.
European gas prices sensitive to LNG supply outages (Qatar) and shipping constraints; volatility elevated.
Refining/product spreads can widen if regional refineries/ports face disruptions (e.g., Ras Tanura, Fujairah).
FX: Risk-off flows can pressure EM FX, especially energy importers in Asia; safe-haven demand may support USD/JPY/CHF.
Gulf currency pegs may hold but local liquidity/credit conditions could tighten if disruption extends.
Rates: Higher energy prices raise near-term inflation risk, complicating DM central bank paths and lifting term premium.
Wider credit spreads possible on geopolitical shock + growth risk; funding costs rise for shipping/energy-linked credits.
Equities: Broad risk-off with underperformance in airlines/transport/chemicals; relative support for energy producers and some defence names.
Higher input and freight costs could hit margins across global industrial and consumer supply chains.

sources.title: US and Israel launch "pre-emptive" attack against Iran
Iran vows to attack any ship trying to pass through Strait of Hormuz
Qatar LNG, Saudi refinery, Israeli oil, gas fields down due to Mideast strikes
Shipping companies divert vessels around Cape of Good Hope after strikes on Iran
Global oil and gas shipping costs surge as Iran vows to close Strait of Hormuz
Iran war enters fourth day in 'smoke and blood' as markets slide
Oil rises as expanding US-Israeli conflict with Iran elevates supply risks
Exclusive: India reduces gas supply to industries after Qatar outage, sources say
Global energy costs soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, oil and gas production
Marine insurers cancel war risk cover, tanker costs to rise as Iran conflict intensifies

events.title: US and Israel launch 'pre-emptive' attack against Iran
events.implication: Direct interstate escalation increases risk to Gulf energy supply and regional shipping routes; markets reprice geopolitical risk premium.

events.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz closed; warns it will attack ships attempting to transit
events.implication: Threats/attempted closure at a key oil/LNG chokepoint create immediate tail-risk for energy prices and global trade flows.

events.title: Iran conflict disrupts global shipping; tankers stranded/damaged
events.implication: Higher freight and war-risk costs propagate into delivered oil/LNG prices and supply-chain delays, amplifying inflation concerns.

events.title: About 10% of global container fleet caught in Hormuz backup, shipping CEO says
events.implication: Container congestion and rerouting adds lead-times and costs, weighing on global cyclicals and supporting risk-off.

events.title: Maersk pauses Suez/Bab el-Mandeb transits; carriers divert around Cape of Good Hope
events.implication: Renewed Red Sea route avoidance tightens shipping capacity and raises freight, adding to imported goods inflation risk.

events.title: Saudi Aramco shuts Ras Tanura refinery after drone strike, source says
events.implication: Refining outage risks product-market tightness and higher crack spreads; adds to oil risk premium.

events.title: Qatar LNG output curtailed after regional strikes, Reuters reports
events.implication: LNG supply interruptions tighten Asia/Europe gas balances; increases gas and power price volatility.

events.title: Israel shuts major gas fields amid regional attacks
events.implication: Eastern Mediterranean gas supply risk can spill into regional power and LNG substitution demand.

events.title: Iraqi Kurdistan output disrupted amid Iran conflict spillover
events.implication: Any sustained disruption reduces marginal supply and raises regional crude differentials.

events.title: Middle East oil and gas shipping costs surge to records
events.implication: Delivered energy costs rise even without outright supply losses; volatility in physical differentials increases.

events.title: Marine insurers cancel/withdraw war-risk cover for some transits
events.implication: Insurance withdrawal can halt trade flows even if routes remain technically open, reinforcing shipping disruptions and raising credit stress.

events.title: Israel says war with Iran will not take years; signals continued operations
events.implication: Public positioning reduces near-term de-escalation probability; keeps risk premium elevated.

events.title: India reduces gas supply to industries after Qatar LNG outage, sources say
events.implication: Demand destruction and rationing indicate real-economy pass-through from supply disruption; negative for growth-sensitive assets.

events.title: Oil rises as conflict elevates supply risks; broader markets slide
events.implication: Higher energy prices and risk-off sentiment tighten financial conditions; supports USD and widens credit spreads.

events.title: Global energy costs soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping and production
events.implication: Sustained cost shock raises inflation risk while weighing on growth; intensifies stagflation-style positioning.

links.from: Maersk pauses Suez/Bab el-Mandeb transits; carriers divert around Cape of Good Hope
links.why: Rerouting raises freight and delays, historically contributing to risk-off and growth concerns.

links.from: Marine insurers cancel/withdraw war-risk cover for some transits
links.to: Middle East oil and gas shipping costs surge to records
links.why: Insurance withdrawal and surcharges directly increase effective shipping costs and reduce available capacity.

links.from: Middle East oil and gas shipping costs surge to records
links.why: Higher delivered energy costs raise inflation uncertainty and can widen credit spreads/funding costs.

links.from: Qatar LNG output curtailed after regional strikes, Reuters reports
links.why: Higher LNG prices and tighter supply can pressure FX for energy importers (e.g., parts of Asia).

links.from: Saudi Aramco shuts Ras Tanura refinery after drone strike, source says
links.why: Refinery outages tighten products supply and reinforce crude/product risk premiums.

links.from: Oil rises as conflict elevates supply risks; broader markets slide
